attempted_job.description: The most likely contract to be classified under the Uniform Commercial Code (UCC) is a contract for
options: The purchase of a commercial property building.
A contract for hiring a live-in nanny.
The purchase of stocks and bonds.
Crops and timber to be severed from the property next summer.
A common carrier delivering a new computer.
The lease of a residential property.
The sale of a personal vehicle.
The sale of an intangible asset.
The contract for a home renovation.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Crops and timber to be severed from the property next summer.

attempted_job.description: One objection to Singer’s theory that he considers is that it:
options: fails to consider the cultural differences in moral norms.
overlooks the role of government and institutions in addressing suffering.
does not do enough to address suffering in other countries besides our own.
does not account for the fact that we have stricter obligations to our loved ones than to strangers.
does not address the issue of moral obligations to non-human animals.
ignores the importance of self-interest in human behavior.
assumes that all individuals have the same capacity to alleviate suffering.
does not provide a clear definition of what constitutes suffering.
requires too drastic a revision to our moral scheme.
attempted_job.correct_answer: requires too drastic a revision to our moral scheme.

attempted_job.description: Does the following series $\sum_{i=0}^{\infty} \frac{n!}{n^2 cos(n)}$ converge?
options: Alternates between positive and negative values

attempted_job.description: Because of a sudden and unanticipated severe shortage of heating fuel, the President has ordered all offices of federal executive agencies to be open only four days per week. The President's order allows an exception to the extent that emergency circumstances require different hours of operation (as in the case of federal hospitals). When Congress enacted the appropriations statute for operating all federal executive agencies, its members assumed that the offices of those agencies would be open five days per week, but Congress did not include such a requirement in its appropriations statute or in any other statute. Is the President's order constitutional?
options: No, because the heads of the various executive agencies have final responsibility for the operation of those agencies' offices.
No, because when they passed the statute appropriating monies for the operation of executive agencies, members of Congress assumed that those agencies' offices would be open five days per week.
Yes, because the Constitution vests the President with plenary authority to direct the administration of all federal agencies in any manner the President deems expedient.
Yes, because the order relates to the management of the executive branch and is not prohibited by any statute.
attempted_job.correct_answer: No, because when they passed the statute appropriating monies for the operation of executive agencies, members of Congress assumed that those agencies' offices would be open five days per week.

attempted_job.description: Carl the clothier owns a large garment factory on an isolated island. Carl's factory is the only source of employment for most of the islanders, and thus Carl acts as a monopsonist. The supply curve for garment workers is given by l = 80w, where l is the number of workers hired and w is their hourly wage. Assume also that Carl's labor demand (marginal revenue product) curve is given by l = 400 - 40MRP_l. How many workers will Carl hire to maximize his profits?
Let x and y be positive integers such that 3x + 7y is divisible by 11. Which of the following must also be divisible by 11?

An automobile with a mass of $1000 \mathrm{~kg}$, including passengers, settles $1.0 \mathrm{~cm}$ closer to the road for every additional $100 \mathrm{~kg}$ of passengers. It is driven with a constant horizontal component of speed $20 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$ over a washboard road with sinusoidal bumps. The amplitude and wavelength of the sine curve are $5.0 \mathrm{~cm}$ and $20 \mathrm{~cm}$, respectively. The distance between the front and back wheels is $2.4 \mathrm{~m}$. Find the amplitude of oscillation of the automobile, assuming it moves vertically as an undamped driven harmonic oscillator. Neglect the mass of the wheels and springs and assume that the wheels are always in contact with the road.
Statement 1 | Every group of order 159 is cyclic. Statement 2 | Every group of order 102 has a nontrivial proper normal subgroup.
options: Cannot be determined, Cannot be determined

attempted_job.description: Estimate the solubility of naphthalene in benzene at room temperature (25°C).
Whatare the differencebetween Marginal Fixed Cost (MFC), Marginal Variable Cost (MVC) and Marginal Total Cost (MC)?
options: MFC decreases as output increases, MVC = 0
MFC = MVC, MC increases as output decreases
MFC = MC, MVC increases with each additional unit produced
MFC increases as output increases, MVC = MC
MFC = 0, MVC increases with each additional unit produced, MC = MFC + MVC

attempted_job.description: Two equal conducting spheres of negligible size are charged with 16.0 × 10^\rule{1em}{1pt}14C and \rule{1em}{1pt}6.4 × 10^\rule{1em}{1pt}14 C, re-spectively, and are placed 20 cm apart. They are then moved to a distance of 50 cm apart. Compare the forces between them in the two positions. The spheres are connected by a thin wire. What force does each now exert on the other?
A monopsonist is identified by one of the following:
options: A wage payment lower than the average revenue product of labor.
Employment level lower than that of a competitive labor market.
A single buyer of labor services.
Employment level greater than that of a competitive labor market.
A wage payment lower than the marginal revenue product of labor.
A wage payment equal to that of a competitive labor market.
A wage payment higher than that of a competitive labor market.
A multiple seller of labor services.
Employment level equal to that of a competitive labor market.
A single seller of labor services.
attempted_job.correct_answer: A wage payment lower than the marginal revenue product of labor.

attempted_job.description: In a business to business context, conflicts of interest can arise in two main ways: conflict of _______ and _______ interests, which might arise, for example, when a firm is hired as a supplier of professional services by another firm or conflict of _________ and ________ interests, such as where an individual's interests may conflict with that of their employer.
Tariffs and quotas
options: sometimes raise and sometimes lower the amount of the product sold domestically.
lower the amount of the product sold domestically.
raise the amount of the product sold domestically.
attempted_job.correct_answer: lower the amount of the product sold domestically.

attempted_job.description: Indicate whether the following economic events represent a change in supply or a change in quantity supplied. a) Cold weather in Florida destroys a great deal of the orange crop. Therefore there are fewer oranges in the country's supermarkets. b) The cost of limes increase. As a result, bar owners raise the price of gin and tonics. c) The price of leather shoes goes up. This induces manufacturers of leather shoes to increase production.
options: a) None of the above, b) Change in supply, c) Change in quantity supplied
a) Change in quantity supplied, b) Change in quantity supplied, c) Change in supply
a) None of the above, b) None of the above, c) None of the above
a) Change in supply, b) Change in quantity supplied, c) None of the above
a) Change in quantity supplied, b) None of the above, c) Change in supply
a) Change in supply, b) Change in quantity supplied, c) Change in supply
a) Change in supply, b) Change in supply, c) Change in quantity supplied
a) Change in quantity supplied, b) Change in supply, c) Change in supply
a) Change in supply, b) Change in quantity supplied, c) Change in quantity supplied
a) Change in quantity supplied, b) Change in supply, c) Change in quantity supplied
attempted_job.correct_answer: a) Change in supply, b) Change in supply, c) Change in quantity supplied

attempted_job.description: State one important reason why Smith's principle of the "invisible hand" does not assure the "perfect functioning," without government intervention, of the U.S. economy today.
options: Smith's principle relies on the assumption of unlimited natural resources
The "invisible hand" is countered by the presence of monopolies and oligopolies
Perfect competition is required for Smith's principle to be completely effective
The principle is based on global, not national, economies
Smith's principle does not account for externalities affecting the economy
Smith's principle assumes all actors have perfect information
The "invisible hand" only works in non-technological markets
Smith's principle only applies to certain markets
attempted_job.correct_answer: Perfect competition is required for Smith's principle to be completely effective

attempted_job.description:
Consider an arbitrage-free securities market model, in which the risk-free interest rate is constant. There are two nondividend-paying stocks whose price processes are:
$S_1(t)=S_1(0)e^{0.1t+0.2Z(t)}$
$S_2(t)=S_2(0)e^{0.125t+0.3Z(t)}$
where $Z(t)$ is a standard Brownian motion ant $t\ge0$. What is the continuously compounded risk-free interest rate?
Ashford's article is meant to address a particular paralysis in the face of
options: the daunting task of solving worldwide economic imbalance.
the impossibility of meeting everyone's basic needs.
having to give up so much of our own wealth in light of Singer's arguments.
having to choose between addressing immediate harm and addressing structural injustice.
the difficult task of prioritizing multiple global issues.
the challenge of implementing sustainable solutions to poverty.
the sheer scope of global poverty.
the overwhelming responsibility of addressing world hunger.
the complexity of addressing environmental issues globally.
attempted_job.correct_answer: the sheer scope of global poverty.

Which of the following is the most likely explanation for these findings?

5.6-9. In Example 5.6-4, compute $P(1.7 \leq Y \leq 3.2)$ with $n=4$ and compare your answer with the normal approximation of this probability.

A previously healthy 15-year-old boy is brought to the emergency department in August 1 hour after the onset of headache, dizziness, nausea, and one episode of vomiting. His symptoms began during the first hour of full-contact football practice in full uniform. He reported feeling weak and faint but did not lose consciousness. He vomited once after drinking water. On arrival, he is diaphoretic. He is not oriented to person, place, or time. His temperature is 39.5°C (103.1°F), pulse is 120/min, respirations are 40/min, and blood pressure is 90/65 mm Hg. Examination, including neurologic examination, shows no other abnormalities. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?
options: Administer oxygen via a nasal cannula
Immerse the patient in an ice water bath
Apply cold compresses to the forehead
Obtain a CT scan of the head
attempted_job.correct_answer: Immerse the patient in an ice water bath

options: a,b in H => a / b in H
a,b in H => a + b in H
a,b in H=> a - b^-1 in H
a in H => a^-1 in H
a,b in H=> a * b in H
a in H => a^2 in H

How many forwarding tables will be indexed to move the datagram from the source to the destination? Answer in [Interfaces, Tables].

Identify the conclusion of the following argument. Remember to remove any conclusion indicators. It is wrong for society to kill a murderer. This follows for the reason that if a murderer is wrong in killing his victim, then society is also wrong in killing the murderer. And a murderer is wrong in killing his victim.
options: A murderer is not wrong in killing his victim.
If a murderer is right in killing his victim, then society is also right in killing the murderer.
And a murderer is wrong in killing his victim.
This does not follow for the reason that if a murderer is wrong in killing his victim, then society is also wrong in killing the murderer.
And a murderer is not wrong in killing his victim.
This follows for the reason that if a murderer is wrong in killing his victim, then society is also wrong in killing the murderer.
If a murderer is wrong in killing his victim, then society is also wrong in killing the murderer.
Society is not wrong in killing the murderer.
A murderer is right in killing his victim.
It is wrong for society to kill a murderer.
attempted_job.correct_answer: It is wrong for society to kill a murderer.

attempted_job.description: John Dalton found water to be 12.5% hydrogen by weight. Calculate the atomic weight of oxygen if Dalton assumed water contained two hydrogen atoms for every three oxygen atoms. Assume 1 H atom weighs 1amu.
Encapsulating Security Payload (ESP) belongs to which Internet Security Protocol?

Carl the clothier owns a large garment factory on an isolated island. Carl's factory is the only source of employment for most of the islanders, and thus Carl acts as a monopsonist. The supply curve for garment workers is given by l = 80w, where l is the number of workers hired and w is their hourly wage. Assume also that Carl's labor demand (marginal revenue product) curve is given by l = 400 - 40MRP_l. How many workers will Carl hire to maximize his profits?

Mouse sperm contain receptor proteins that bind to a glycoprotein (ZP3) in the zona pellucida of mouse eggs. Binding of ZP3 receptors to ZP3 initiates the sperm’s acrosomal reaction. All of the following experimental observations would be expected EXCEPT:
options: Injecting eggs with antibodies that bind to ZP3 blocks in vitro fertilization.
Incubating sperm with purified ZP3 prior to mixing sperm and eggs blocks in vitro fertilization.
Incubating eggs with antibodies that bind to ZP3 blocks in vitro fertilization.
Coating synthetic beads with a different glycoprotein, ZP2, fails to lead to sperm aggregation on the beads.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Injecting eggs with antibodies that bind to ZP3 blocks in vitro fertilization.

attempted_job.description: A charge distribution that is spherically symmetric but not uniform radially produces an electric field of magnitude $E=K r^4$, directed radially outward from the center of the sphere. Here $r$ is the radial distance from that center, and $K$ is a constant. What is the volume density $\rho$ of the charge distribution?
An embryologist studying the development of a vertebrate organism from the zygote stage to fetus would be justified in drawing which of the following conclusions?
options: The development of a vertebrate organism from the zygote stage to fetus does not follow a consistent pattern.
The development of a vertebrate organism from the zygote stage to fetus displays a random pattern of evolutionary history.
The zygote stage of a vertebrate organism is identical to the fetus stage.
Early embryos display identical features of their class and order, but not their species.
An early human embryo has features in common with early fish and avian embryos.
Early embryos display identical features of their class, order, and species.
A human embryo displays features of adult fish and birds in the course of its development.
An early human embryo has features that are identical to adult fish and avian features.
A vertebrate organism develops identical features of its class, order, and species only during the fetus stage.

attempted_job.description: Which of the following statements is correct concerning the "reasonable person" standard in tort law?
options: The reasonable person standard applies only to people with a high level of education.
The reasonable person standard applies only to people without disabilities.
The reasonable person standard varies from person to person.
The reasonable person standard requires that a person act as would the average person in the community.
A person with a mental disability must act as would a person with the same mental disability.
A person with a physical disability must act as would a reasonable person with the same disability.
The reasonable person standard focuses on the defendant's subjective mental state rather than on the defendant's behavior
The reasonable person standard is based solely on the defendant's age.
A person with a mental disability is not subject to the reasonable person standard.
attempted_job.correct_answer: A person with a physical disability must act as would a reasonable person with the same disability.

attempted_job.description: Which of the following statements about the immune system is NOT correct?
options: Innate immunity, also known as the adaptive immune response, relies on circulating phagocytes engulfing foreign substances.
Adaptive immunity is a slower response than innate immunity.
Innate immunity activates a humoral response.
Dendritic cells are antigen presenting cells.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Innate immunity, also known as the adaptive immune response, relies on circulating phagocytes engulfing foreign substances.

attempted_job.description: The ground level of $\mathrm{Cl}$ is ${ }^2 \mathrm{P}_{3 / 2}$ and a ${ }^2 \mathrm{P}_{1 / 2}$ level lies $881 \mathrm{~cm}^{-1}$ above it. Calculate the electronic contribution to the molar Gibbs energy of $\mathrm{Cl}$ atoms at  $500 \mathrm{~K}$.

A researcher grows a population of ferns in her laboratory. She notices, after a few generations, a new variant that has a distinct phenotype. When she tries to breed the original phenotype with the new one, no offspring are produced. When she breeds the new variants, however, offspring that look like the new variant result. Which of the following could possibly characterize the new variant?
A water bed sells for $330 cash or $40 down and $10.50 a month for 36 months. Find the annual interest rate charged.
The bandwidth of an analog signal is 4kHz. An A/D converter is used to convert the signal from analog to digital. What is the minimum sampling rate for eliminating the aliasing problem? (in kHz)

Shortly after breaking up with the defendant, a woman began dating the victim. The defendant, who still loved the woman, hated the victim. During a holiday weekend, the woman and the victim arranged to go camping in a federal park. The defendant and his friend decided to beat up the victim while he and the woman were on their camping trip. They went to the campsite where the woman and the victim were staying, but they couldn't find the couple, who were hiking in the woods. Subsequently, the defendant was arrested and charged with conspiracy to commit an assault in a federal park. At trial, the defendant testified that he didn't know he was in a federal park. Moreover, he stated that if he had known, he would never have agreed to the crime. If the jury believes the defendant, he should be found
options: guilty, because the location of the crime does not affect the intent to commit the crime.
guilty, because agreeing to commit a crime is enough to be charged with conspiracy.
not guilty, because he did not agree to commit a crime in a federal park.
guilty, because federal conspiracy laws require only an intent to commit a prohibited act, but do not require a knowledge of the surrounding circumstances.
not guilty, because he did not actually find the victim to carry out the assault.
not guilty, because he didn't have the specific intent to commit the crime of assault in a federal park.
not guilty, because he lacked the knowledge that his actions were illegal.
not guilty, because he did not actually commit the assault.
guilty, because federal conspiracy laws do not require the mental retainment ofjurisdictional requirements.
guilty, because his testimony shows that he had an intent to harm the victim.
attempted_job.correct_answer: guilty, because federal conspiracy laws do not require the mental retainment ofjurisdictional requirements.

attempted_job.description: The diffusion coefficient for serum globulin at 20° in a dilute aqueous salt solution is 4.0 × 10^-11 m^2 s^-1. If the molecules are assumed to be spherical, calculate their molecular weight. Given \etaH_2O = 0.001005 Pascal sec at 20° andѵ= 0.75 (cm^3/g) for the protein.

A person was taking a long hike on a trail through a forest in a state park. As the hiker came around a bend in the trail, he suddenly saw a hunter standing next to the trail, looking in the other direction. The hunter was holding a rifle. The hunter turned, causing the rifle to be pointed at the hiker. The hiker felt sure that he was going to get shot, but the gun did not go off. The hiker asserts a claim for assault against the hunter. In his action, the most likely result is that the hiker will
options: recover, if the hiker can prove negligence on the hunter's part.
recover, if the hunter knew the hiker was there when he turned.
recover, because the hunter's rifle was pointed directly at him.
recover, if the hiker feared for his life.
not recover, if the hunter did not intend to shoot the hiker.
not recover, unless the hiker can prove the hunter saw him.
not recover, if the hiker failed to make his presence known to the hunter.
recover, because the hunter's decision to carry the gun was voluntary.
not recover, unless the hunter intended to scare the hiker.
attempted_job.correct_answer: not recover, unless the hunter intended to scare the hiker.

Which one of the following criticisms of the Dickey-Fuller/Engle-Granger approach to dealing with cointegrated variables is overcome by the Engle-Yoo (EY) procedure?
options: In the context of small samples, Dickey Fuller tests are prone to conclude that there is a unit root in a series when there is not
The Engle-Granger (EG) approach can only detect up to one cointegrating relationship even though there could be more than one
The variables are treated asymmetrically in the cointegrating tests
It is not possible to perform tests about the cointegrating relationship
attempted_job.correct_answer: It is not possible to perform tests about the cointegrating relationship

Assume that all gases are perfect and that data refer to 298.15 K unless otherwise stated. Consider a system consisting of $2.0 \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g})$, initially at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and $10 \mathrm{~atm}$ and confined to a cylinder of cross-section $10.0 \mathrm{~cm}^2$. It is allowed to expand adiabatically against an external pressure of 1.0 atm until the piston has moved outwards through $20 \mathrm{~cm}$. Assume that carbon dioxide may be considered a perfect gas with $C_{V, \mathrm{~m}}=28.8 \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~K}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ and calculate $\Delta U$.

A state has recently enacted a statute requiring all prospective voters in state elections who wish to write-in a candidate to print the candidate's full name, and designate the office for which the candidate is running. The statute provides that such information must be written on the ballot in ink inan appropriate space. A write-in candidate is a German-American and is of the opinion that he needs a large turnout of German voters in order to win the election. As a result, his campaign manager decides to mail to every registered German voter a tear-off sticker, which bears the candidate's name and office for which he is running. Since many native German people are not proficient in reading and writing English, the campaign manager believes that many of the voters will have difficulty writing the candidate's name and office on the ballot. As a result, the campaign manager has mounted an extensive media campaign to inform voters on how to apply the stickers to the ballot. Five months prior to the election an election official notifies the candidate's campaign committee that the tear-off stickers do not comply with the state statute. In her letter, the official explains that state election officials are of the opinion that it is necessary for potential voters to write the candidate's name in ink. Therefore, she concludes that the stickers do not comply with statutory requirements. Three weeks later, the candidate filed suit in federal district court against state election officials, claiming that their interpretation of the state statute violates the U. S. Constitution. Which of the following sets forth the strongest constitutional argument the candidate could make against the interpretation of the statute by the state officials?
options: It unreasonably discriminates against German voters who lack a proficiency in the English language.
It unreasonably interferes with the exclusive federal election power as embodied in the Fifteenth Amendment.
It goes against the spirit of the Twenty-fourth Amendment by effectively imposing a "literacy tax."
It unreasonably discriminates against non-English speaking voters in general.
It violates the Equal Protection Clause by treating write-in candidates differently.
It unreasonably discriminates against write-in candidates for public office.
It unreasonably limits the means by which a candidate can campaign, infringing on democratic practices.
It unreasonably restricts freedom of speech as guaranteed by the First Amendment.
It unreasonably interferes with the 1965 Voting Rights Act outlawing literacy tests.
It interferes with the right to vote as guaranteed by the Nineteenth Amendment.
attempted_job.correct_answer: It unreasonably discriminates against write-in candidates for public office.

Statement 1 is True, Statement 2 is True

options: The development of a vertebrate organism from the zygote stage to fetus does not follow a consistent pattern.
The development of a vertebrate organism from the zygote stage to fetus displays a random pattern of evolutionary history.
The zygote stage of a vertebrate organism is identical to the fetus stage.
Early embryos display identical features of their class and order, but not their species.

Let F: K \times R \to MF:K×R→M be a secure PRF. For m \in Mm∈M define E(k,m) = \big[ r \gets R,\ \text{output } \big(r,\ F(k,r) \oplus m\big)\ \big]E(k,m)=[r←R, output (r, F(k,r)⊕m) ] Is EE symantically secure under CPA?
options: It depends on the size of R
Yes, but only if R is large enough so r never repeats (w.h.p)
Yes, but only if M is small enough so m never repeats (w.h.p)
No, unless F is a weak PRF
No, unless F is a strong PRF
Yes, but only if K is large enough so k never repeats (w.h.p)
It depends on the size of K
No, there is always a CPA attack on this system
It depends on what F is used
Yes, whenever F is a secure PRF
attempted_job.correct_answer: Yes, but only if R is large enough so r never repeats (w.h.p)

attempted_job.description: What is the pressure inside a boiler which contains liquid water and steam at a temperature of 225°F. Assume that the enthalpy of vaporization of water at 212°F, 1 atm is 970.3 Btu/lbm and the specific volume of the vapor and liquid phases are 26.80 and 0.01672 ft^3/lbm respectively.

A model of a proposed low-speed crop-dusting plane is to be tested in a wind tunnel capable of maintaining velocities of 264 ft/s, using atmospheric air. The prototype plane has a design wing span of 27 ft and a proposed speed of 88 ft/s. In order to preserve dynamic similarity, what should be the wing span of the model if the wind tunnel is to operate at full speed? Assume the air at these low velocities to be essentially incompressible. In addition, assume that the temperature of atmospheric air and the temperature of the wind-tunnel air are the same.

The gene frequency for theRhallele among American whites is 0.028. Among American blacks, the frequency is 0.446. Among East African blacks, the frequency of theRh allele is 0.630. Determine the degree of gene exchange (m) between the American whites and blacks.

This question refers to the following information.
"To slacken the tempo would mean falling behind. And those who fall behind get beaten. But we do not want to be beaten. No, we refuse to be beaten! One feature of the history of old Russia was the continual beatings she suffered because of her backwardness. She was beaten by the Mongol khans. She was beaten by the Turkish beys. She was beaten by the Swedish feudal lords. She was beaten by the Polish and Lithuanian gentry. She was beaten by the British and French capitalists. She was beaten by the Japanese barons. All beat her––because of her backwardness, because of her military backwardness, cultural backwardness, political backwardness, industrial backwardness, agricultural backwardness. They beat her because it was profitable and could be done with impunity. You remember the words of the pre-revolutionary poet: "You are poor and abundant, mighty and impotent, Mother Russia." Those gentlemen were quite familiar with the verses of the old poet. They beat her, saying: "You are abundant," so one can enrich oneself at your expense. They beat her, saying: "You are poor and impotent," so you can be beaten and plundered with impunity. Such is the law of the exploiters––to beat the backward and the weak. It is the jungle law of capitalism. You are backward, you are weak––therefore you are wrong; hence you can be beaten and enslaved. You are mighty––therefore you are right; hence we must be wary of you.
That is why we must no longer lag behind."
Joseph Stalin, speech delivered at the first All-Union Conference of Leading Personnel of Socialist Industry, February 4, 1931
The reference to the "continual beatings" most directly supports which of the following decisions in later Soviet foreign policy?
options: The declaration of war against Japan at the end of World War II
The decision to build the Berlin Wall
The imposition of the Brezhnev Doctrine against anti-Soviet protesters in Europe
The signing of the Molotov-Ribbentrop Pact
The diplomatic recognition of the People's Republic of China
The creation of the Warsaw Pact
attempted_job.correct_answer: The creation of the Warsaw Pact

What were the factors that contributed to the failureof agricultureto share in the prosperity that other segments of theeconomy did immediately after the post-war depres-sion in1920?
options: Decreased technological advances leading to lower production efficiency
An increase in the cost of agricultural inputs without a corresponding rise in product prices
High domestic demand for farm products
A surplus in foreign markets leading to decreased exports
High import tariffs on foreign agricultural goods
Technological advances, import tariffs, low domestic demand for farm products, increased supply
A major shift to service-based industries reducing the workforce in agriculture
Increased foreign demand for US goods
attempted_job.correct_answer: Technological advances, import tariffs, low domestic demand for farm products, increased supply

attempted_job.description: A simple harmonic oscillator consists of a 100-g mass attached to a spring whose force constant is $10^4 \mathrm{dyne} / \mathrm{cm}$. The mass is displaced $3 \mathrm{~cm}$ and released from rest. Calculate the natural frequency $\nu_0$.
Electrical stimulation of the brain for the treatment of chronic pain
options: is not effective and can lead to further health complications
is of no practical significance, since it can only provide temporary relief.
may produce its effects by suppressing the central nervous system
is of no practical significance, since stimulation-produced analgesia has been demonstrated only in rats
may produce its effects by blocking the pain signals to the brain
is generally effective, but medically ill-advised because such stimulation has aversive properties
is generally effective, but can lead to addiction
may produce its effects by promoting the release of the neurotransmitter norepinephrine
may produce its effects by promoting the release of the neurotransmitter serotonin
attempted_job.correct_answer: is of no practical significance, since stimulation-produced analgesia has been demonstrated only in rats

attempted_job.description: A suspected drug dealer was arrested after selling three grams of cocaine to undercover police agents. He was subsequently convicted in state court of possession with intent to distribute narcotics. Following his conviction, the drug dealer was sentenced to a prison term of 10 years. After being sentenced, the drug dealer's attorney appealed the conviction, citing jury misconduct. While his appeal was pending, the drug dealer was then indicted by both a federal and a state grand jury for conspiracy to distribute the same cocaine. The drug dealer's attorney filed motions to dismiss each of the new indictments on the grounds that they violate double jeopardy. In all likelihood, double jeopardy requires the dismissal of
options: the federal indictment, if the state indictment is dismissed.
both indictments, if the appeal is successful.
the state indictment, if the federal court conviction is upheld.
the federal indictment, but not the state indictment.
the state indictment, but not the federal indictment.
the federal indictment, if the state court conviction is upheld.
both indictments, if the appeal is unsuccessful.
the state indictment, if the federal indictment is dismissed.

attempted_job.description: The United States produces rice in a competitive market. With free trade the world price is lower than the domestic price. What must be true?

The United States begins to export rice to make up for a domestic shortage.
The United States begins to import rice to eliminate a domestic surplus.
The United States begins to export rice to eliminate a domestic surplus.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The United States begins to import rice to make up for a domestic shortage.

To account for short-term market fluctuations
To prepare for upcoming financial crises
To correct for data collection errors
To align with fiscal policy changes

attempted_job.description: Operant extinction would NOT be the treatment-of-choice when:
options: the behavior has been reinforced on a continuous schedule.
the behavior is not causing significant distress or dysfunction.
the behavior is a part of the individual's cultural or religious practices.
an alternative behavior cannot be identified.
a temporary increase in the behavior cannot be tolerated.
the baseline levels of the behavior cannot be established.
attempted_job.correct_answer: a temporary increase in the behavior cannot be tolerated.

attempted_job.description: Crowding out
options: occurs when interest rates fall due to government borrowing.
occurs when consumers and firms spend less offsetting expansionary fiscal policy.
occurs when the government reduces its spending in response to inflation.
is a consequence of contractionary fiscal policy.
is one reason fiscal policy is so effective.
is when expansionary fiscal policy leads to a decrease in interest rates.
happens when the government increases taxes to reduce the budget deficit.
causes the aggregate demand curve to shift to the right.
attempted_job.correct_answer: occurs when consumers and firms spend less offsetting expansionary fiscal policy.

attempted_job.description: Which is NOT a criticism of Piaget’s theory of cognitive development?
options: Piaget placed too much emphasis on the role of culture and social interactions in children’s cognitive development
Piaget's theory does not consider the impact of digital technology on cognitive development
Cognitive development is more gradual and continuous rather than abrupt and stagelike as Piaget thought
Piaget's theory does not explain how cognitive development continues into adulthood
Piaget's theory ignores the role of genetics in cognitive development
Children actually have certain cognitive capabilities at particular ages that Piaget did not observe because he designed experiments that were too difficult
Piaget's theory is too focused on biological factors and not enough on environmental influences
Piaget may have underestimated young children’s competence and overestimated adolescents’ cognitive abilities
Piaget's theory underestimates the cognitive abilities of infants
Piaget's theory overstates the role of language in cognitive development
attempted_job.correct_answer: Piaget placed too much emphasis on the role of culture and social interactions in children’s cognitive development
